questions.question: A cylindrical can has a radius of 3 cm and a height of 10 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h. V = 3.14 × 3² × 10 = 3.14 × 9 × 10 = 282.6 cm³.

questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 4 m × 5 m × 6 m. If you double each dimension, what is the ratio of the new volume to the original volume?
questions.explanation: Original volume = 4 × 5 × 6 = 120 m³. New dimensions: 8 × 10 × 12, new volume = 960 m³. Ratio = 960 ÷ 120 = 8 (or 2³).

questions.question: A cone has a base radius of 5 inches and a height of 12 inches. What is its volume in cubic inches? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
A sphere has a radius of 6 cm. Which is greater: its volume or 400 cm³? By how much? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 20 square feet and a depth (length) of 9 feet. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = base area × depth. V = 20 × 9 = 180 ft³. No need to know the triangle's specific dimensions.

questions.question: A cylindrical water tank holds 1,000 cubic meters of water. If the radius is 5 meters, what is the height in meters? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
